title: Occurrence-Level Validation
breadcrumbs: Validation at Field, Key, Occurrence, or Entity Level
text: Occurrence-Level Validation An occurrence may contain both fields and inner entities. It is possible to select between validating an occurrence with, or without, its inner entities. The conditions for occurrence validation are either: The data of one or more fields in the occurrence has been modified, and the occurrence has not yet been validated successfully, or Validation is demanded for the occurrence by setting $occcheck . Excluding Inner Entities Validating an occurrence without validating the inner entities can be initiated in the following ways: By the validateocc ProcScript statement. By an event that changes focus, such as going to the next occurrence with the ^NEXT_OCC structure editor function. The process for validating an occurrence without validating the inner entities is as follows: Uniface validates the primary key. Uniface validates all candidate keys. Uniface validates all non-key fields. Uniface activates the validate trigger for the occurrence. Including the Inner Entities Uniface can be instructed to validate an occurrence including its inner entities in the following ways: By the validate/o ProcScript statement As part of the validation of a higher level The process for validating an occurrence including its inner entities is as follows: Uniface validates all inner entities. Uniface validates the outer occurrence using validateocc . Related Links $occcheck Return or set the requirement for checking an occurrence. validateocc Validate an occurrence. Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). Was this topic helpful?
